title: strychnos spinosa subsp. lokua
intro: Strychnos spinosa subsp. lokua is a plant species belonging to the family Loganiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Africa. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Strychnos spinosa subsp. lokua include its use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by boiling the roots and leaves in water, which can be used as a decoction or infused as a tea for its medicinal properties.
